# Recommended GitHub Labels

Use these labels to keep issues and pull requests searchable and triageable.

| Label | Purpose |
| --- | --- |
| `bug` | Confirmed or suspected defect |
| `feature` | New capability proposal |
| `enhancement` | Improvement to existing behavior |
| `documentation` | Docs-only work |
| `frontend` | React, TypeScript, UI, map, or browser behavior |
| `backend` | FastAPI, services, MAVSDK integration |
| `api` | HTTP contracts, models, endpoint behavior |
| `database` | SQLite schema, persistence, migrations |
| `testing` | Automated tests, validation scripts, QA process |
| `high priority` | Important or release-blocking work |
| `low priority` | Useful but non-urgent work |
| `good first issue` | Small, well-scoped contributor task |
| `help wanted` | Maintainer-approved request for external contribution |

Recommended practice:

- Apply at least one area label such as `frontend`, `backend`, `api`, or `database`.
- Apply one type label such as `bug`, `feature`, `enhancement`, or `documentation`.
- Use priority labels sparingly.
